main.description: An invitation — a capability — to start a new story on branchline. Lives in the repo of the current holder: the trusted grantor for a root grant, or the previous holder for a pass. Root grants have no `previous`; they are authoritative only when written to a repo whose DID is configured as a trusted grantor in the AppView. Passes carry `previous` as a strong ref to the predecessor grant and must be written to a repo owned by that predecessor's `recipient`. A grant is single-use: it may be spent exactly once by either (a) being gifted onward — another seedGrant referencing this one as `previous` — or (b) being redeemed — an ink.branchline.bud whose `grant` strong-refs this one. The AppView enforces single-use via a unique constraint on the committed grant uri at the moment a chain is redeemed into a bud. Until that commitment, grants are revocable: deleting a pass (or the root) from its holding repo invalidates any descendants that have not yet been committed into a bud, which is the mechanism holders use to change their minds about who they gifted a seed to.

previous.description: Strong ref to the predecessor grant this one was passed from. Absent on root grants minted by a trusted grantor. When present, the record owner's DID must equal the predecessor's `recipient` — enforced by the AppView's chain validator.

recipient.description: DID authorized to spend this grant — either by planting it (publishing an ink.branchline.bud whose `grant` references this record) or by gifting it onward (writing a new ink.branchline.seedGrant whose `previous` references this record) to that DID's own PDS.

note.description: Optional prose from the grantor to the recipient — a dedication, a prompt, the reason the seed was gifted.
